endpoints.description: GameSpot WordPress REST search endpoint for review candidates. Use browser-context fetch because direct external HTTP can hit Cloudflare. The compact output returns candidate review titles, article URLs, and self API URLs.
strategy_notes: Build the search query from the task title plus the word review; keep subtype=reviews and per_page=20.
Pick the candidate whose title best matches the requested game/article, then call its apiUrl/self href.
Do not use tracking endpoints such as beacon.wikia-services.com as data APIs.

endpoints.description: GameSpot WordPress REST review detail endpoint. Use browser-context fetch to compact review scorecard data such as title, reviewer, score, pros, cons, verdict, and article URL without relying on the heavy article DOM.
strategy_notes: Use the apiUrl/self href returned by the review search endpoint; add _embed=1 when author details are needed.
If score or pros/cons are absent from REST detail, open the article URL once and extract visible scorecard fields.
Return missing scorecard fields as not shown only after the REST detail and one article HTML check do not expose them.

endpoints.description: GameSpot review article HTML fallback. Use browser-context fetch only when REST detail is insufficient, then compact JSON-LD/body text into scorecard-like fields.
strategy_notes: Use this after REST search/detail, not before.
Extract score, reviewer, The Good, The Bad, and verdict from JSON-LD or visible article text.
